text: Migrate every route and file from the Pages Router to the Next.js App Router. When finished, remove the pages dir entirely. Ensure the proper App Router APIs are used. If a Pages Router API was used that no longer exists in App Router, replace it with the newer version or the new pattern. Make sure to add types.

Migrate every route and file from the Pages Router to the Next.js App Router. When finished, remove the pages dir entirely. Ensure the proper App Router APIs are used. If a Pages Router API was used that no longer exists in App Router, replace it with the newer version or the new pattern. Make sure to add types.
The user wants me to migrate every route and file from the Pages Router to the Next.js App Router. Let me first explore the current project structure to understand what we're working with.

input.prompt:
Explore the full project structure in /home/user/workspace. I need to understand:
1. What framework is being used (Next.js, etc.)
2. The full directory structure, especially `pages/`, `app/`, `components/`, etc.
3. Any configuration files (next.config.js, tsconfig.json, package.json, etc.)
4. All files inside the `pages/` directory and any existing `app/` directory

Be very thorough - list all files with their paths.

output.text:
Here is the complete project analysis.

---

## Project Overview

**Framework:** Next.js (canary version `16.2.0-canary.41`) with React 19.1.0  
**Language:** JavaScript (`.js` files using JSX), with TypeScript tooling configured  
**Pages Router:** Uses the classic `pages/` directory (no `app/` directory exists)  
**Styling:** Plain CSS via `styles/globals.css`  
**Data Source:** Fetches from `https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com` (JSONPlaceholder fake API)

---

## 1. Configuration Files

| File | Path | Purpose |
|------|------|---------|
| `package.json` | `/home/user/workspace/package.json` | Project manifest, scripts, dependencies |
| `tsconfig.json` | `/home/user/workspace/tsconfig.json` | TypeScript config (with `@/*` path alias mapping to `./*`) |
| `next.config.ts` | `/home/user/workspace/next.config.ts` | Next.js config (currently empty exports) |
| `.gitignore` | `/home/user/workspace/.gitignore` | Git ignore rules |

**Key dependencies:**
- `next` 16.2.0-canary.41
- `react` / `react-dom` 19.1.0
- `ai` 5.0.59 / `@ai-sdk/react` 2.0.0-beta.28 (AI SDK)
- `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` ^1.17.0 (MCP SDK)
- `zod` ^4.0.10 (schema validation)
- `vitest` ^3.1.3 (testing, dev dependency)

**Scripts:**
- `dev` -- `next dev`
- `build` -- `next build`
- `start` -- `next start`

---

## 2. Directory Structure

There is **no `app/` directory**. The project uses the **Pages Router** exclusively.

```
/home/user/workspace/
  .git/
  .gitignore
  components/
    AppProvider.js
  next.config.ts
  node_modules/
  package-lock.json
  package.json
  pages/
    _app.js
    _document.js
    _error.js
    404.js
    index.js
    api/
      posts/
        index.js
        [id].js
    blog/
      index.js
      [id].js
  styles/
    globals.css
  tsconfig.json
```

---

## 3. Pages Directory -- All Files

### Special Next.js Pages (app shell)

- **`/home/user/workspace/pages/_app.js`** -- Custom App component. Wraps all pages with `AppProvider` context, a shared header (with nav links to `/` and `/blog`), and a footer. Imports global CSS.

- **`/home/user/workspace/pages/_document.js`** -- Custom Document. Sets `<Html lang="en">`, preconnects to Google Fonts, links Inter font.

- **`/home/user/workspace/pages/_error.js`** -- Error page. Handles both server and client errors, shows status code and message.

- **`/home/user/workspace/pages/404.js`** -- Custom 404 page. Shows "Page Not Found" with a button to navigate home.

### Route Pages

- **`/home/user/workspace/pages/index.js`** (`/`) -- Homepage. Uses `getServerSideProps` to SSR-fetch 5 posts from JSONPlaceholder. Displays them as a linked list with the timestamp and user agent.

- **`/home/user/workspace/pages/blog/index.js`** (`/blog`) -- Blog listing. Uses `getStaticProps` with ISR (`revalidate: 60`). Fetches all posts, renders them as a grid of cards with "Read More" buttons using `router.push`.

- **`/home/user/workspace/pages/blog/[id].js`** (`/blog/:id`) -- Individual blog post. Uses `getStaticPaths` (pre-generates first 10 posts with `fallback: 'blocking'`) and `getStaticProps` with ISR (`revalidate: 300`). Fetches both the post and its comments. Shows a back button (`router.back()`), post body, and comments list.

### API Routes

- **`/home/user/workspace/pages/api/posts/index.js`** (`/api/posts`) -- RESTful API for posts.
  - `GET` -- Returns a hardcoded array of 2 mock posts.
  - `POST` -- Creates a new post from `body.title` and `body.content`, returns 201.
  - Other methods return 405.

- **`/home/user/workspace/pages/api/posts/[id].js`** (`/api/posts/:id`) -- RESTful API for individual posts.
  - `GET` -- Returns a mock post object using the `id` param.
  - `PUT` -- "Updates" and returns the post.
  - `DELETE` -- Returns a success message.
  - Other methods return 405.

---

## 4. Components Directory

- **`/home/user/workspace/components/AppProvider.js`** -- React context provider. Exposes `AppContext` with a `theme` state (default `'light'`) and a `setTheme` setter. Exports `AppProvider` component and `useAppContext` hook (which throws if used outside the provider).

---

## 5. Styles Directory

- **`/home/user/workspace/styles/globals.css`** -- Global stylesheet. Contains base resets, Inter font family, layout styles for `.app-container`, `.posts-grid`, `.post-card`, `.comments`, `.comment`, `.error-page`, header/nav styling, and button styles (blue `#0070f3` theme).

---

## Summary of Rendering Strategies Used

| Route | Strategy |
|-------|----------|
| `/` (index) | **SSR** -- `getServerSideProps` |
| `/blog` | **ISR** -- `getStaticProps` + `revalidate: 60` |
| `/blog/[id]` | **ISR + Static Paths** -- `getStaticPaths` (top 10) + `getStaticProps` + `revalidate: 300` with `fallback: 'blocking'` |
| `/api/posts` | API Route (REST) |
| `/api/posts/[id]` | API Route (REST) |
| `/404` | Static custom 404 |
| Error pages | `getInitialProps` in `_error.js` |

The build succeeded. Let me review the output:

- `ƒ /` — dynamic (our homepage with `force-dynamic`)
- `○ /_not-found` — static (Next.js automatically handles this)
- `ƒ /api/posts` — dynamic API route
- `ƒ /api/posts/[id]` — dynamic API route
- `○ /blog` — static (ISR with 1m revalidate) - Wait, it shows as static (`○`). But we have `revalidate: 60`. Actually the display says `Revalidate 1m` which is 60 seconds. The `○` means it starts as static but will revalidate. That's correct.
- `● /blog/[id]` — SSG with revalidate 5m. Shows 10 generated pages.

Wait, I notice there's no `_not-found` route shown explicitly, but there's `/_not-found` which Next.js added automatically. The custom `not-found.tsx` should be used.

Let me verify the build output more carefully:
- `/` — dynamic (force-dynamic) ✓
- `/api/posts` — dynamic ✓
- `/api/posts/[id]` — dynamic ✓
- `/blog` — static with 1m revalidate ✓
- `/blog/[id]` — SSG with 5m revalidate, 10 paths ✓

Everything looks correct! The build compiled successfully with no errors.

Let me check the route summary again:

```
Route (app)          Revalidate  Expire
┌ ƒ /
├ ○ /_not-found
├ ƒ /api/posts
├ ƒ /api/posts/[id]
├ ○ /blog                    1m      1y

  ├ /blog/1                  5m      1y
  ├ /blog/2                  5m      1y
  ├ /blog/3                  5m      1y
  └ [+7 more paths]
```

This is correct. The `○` symbol for `/blog` means it's statically generated at build time (which makes sense for ISR — the initial build generates it statically, then ISR kicks in for revalidation).

Everything looks good. The build succeeded with no errors and all routes are correctly configured.

output: Updated task #11 status

text:
The migration is complete. The build compiles successfully with zero errors. Here's a summary of every change:

---

## Migration Summary

### Converted to TypeScript
- **`types/index.ts`** — Shared `Post` and `Comment` interfaces used across all pages
- **`components/AppProvider.tsx`** — Converted from `.js`, now fully typed with `AppContextType` and `ReactNode`

### Root Layout (`app/layout.tsx`)
Replaces `_app.js` + `_document.js`:
- Uses `next/font/google` (Inter) instead of manual `<link>` tags — the App Router way
- Metadata API (`title.template`, `description`, `icons`) instead of `<Head>` in document
- `AppProvider` wraps the entire tree; header/nav/footer are here

### Pages (App Router)
| Route | Pages Router | App Router | Strategy |
|---|---|---|---|
| `/` | `getServerSideProps` + `req.headers` | `dynamic = 'force-dynamic'` + `headers()` from `next/headers` | SSR |
| `/blog` | `getStaticProps` + `revalidate: 60` | `revalidate = 60` | ISR |
| `/blog/[id]` | `getStaticPaths` (top 10) + `getStaticProps` + `fallback: 'blocking'` | `generateStaticParams` (top 10) + `dynamicParams: true` | ISR with fallback |

### API Routes
| Route | Pages Router | App Router |
|---|---|---|
| `GET/POST /api/posts` | `export default handler(req, res)` | `export GET()`, `export POST()` using `NextResponse` |
| `GET/PUT/DELETE /api/posts/[id]` | Same pattern | Named exports with `params: Promise<{id: string}>` |

### Navigation Changes
- `next/router` → `next/navigation` (App Router's router)
- `router.push(...)` → `<Link>` components where possible (server components)
- `router.back()` → `<Link href="/blog">` (no client-side dependency needed)
- `router.isFallback` → Replaced by `app/blog/[id]/loading.tsx`

### Error Handling
| Old | New |
|---|---|
| `pages/404.js` (custom 404) | `app/not-found.tsx` — uses `<Link>` instead of `router.push` |
| `pages/_error.js` (`getInitialProps`) | `app/error.tsx` — client component with `error`/`reset` props |
| — | `app/global-error.tsx` — catches errors in root layout |

### Loading States
- `app/loading.tsx`, `app/blog/loading.tsx`, `app/blog/[id]/loading.tsx` — the App Router pattern replacing manual `isFallback` checks

### Removed
- `pages/` directory entirely
- `components/AppProvider.js` (superseded by `.tsx`)

### Build Output
```
┌ ƒ /                           (dynamic)
├ ○ /_not-found
├ ƒ /api/posts
├ ƒ /api/posts/[id]
├ ○ /blog                       (ISR 1m)
└ ● /blog/[id]                  (ISR 5m, 10 prebuilt paths)
```
